text: Hi everyone! Today we're doing a seemingly complicated braided updo that is actually
really really easy to accomplish. Let's go ahead and get started. I'm going to apply
some mousse to keep all my layers in because it's really choppy. This particular one is
my finesse but you can use whatever you have. I'm going to use about this much. We're going
to tie our hair up in a half ponytail but leave a small section out on the side. We
go in on this side from right above the ear and over here from where my temple is. I'm
going to tie it up like this. Now you want to dig a hole and turn the ponytail and push
it through like that. Very easy. With the hair that you've left aside you want to do
a simple braid. Now we're going to tie our hair up. We're going to tie our hair up like
this. You want to secure this with an elastic as well. I'm just going to take this braid
and wrap this around the base of that ponytail and pin it underneath right here with two
bobby pins crossing each other. I'm going to tie it up like this. I'm going to tie it
up like this. I'm going to divide the bottom into three parts and braid it toward the
top. Try your best to incorporate all the small pieces of the shorter hair into the
braid. I'm going to do it as tight as you can. Just secure the ends with an elastic.
Simply taking the braid and tucking it into this hole here. Then just pin that down.
Usually I can just use a smaller bobby pin to hold everything down, but I thought I would
use a huge one. So you can see where this thing is going. I really want to pick up a
little bit of hair at the end before the elastic. You're going toward the top to make sure that
toward the top right, just push it back down into the hole and that's going to help you hold the
braid up there. I'm going to go in with the smaller one now to give it an extra hold.
Doing the same thing. Lastly we're just going to go in with some hairspray to get rid of all the
flyaways. So this is it. Feel free to accessorize if you want. Here is the final look. Looks kind
of complicated but it's actually super easy to do.
Hopefully you enjoyed the tutorial. Let me know how it works out for you. Thanks so much for watching and I'll see you soon.
